critic_raw:
The hybrid abnormality score combining PSNR and L2 between queries and memory items is a compound metric with no stated weighting scheme. The relative contribution of each component is unspecified, and sensitivity to this weighting is undiscussed — different weightings could produce arbitrarily different classification thresholds.

The test-time memory update strategy is circularly dependent: updating memory items on test-time frames assumed to be normal requires the model to already distinguish normal from anomalous before classification has occurred. If the updater uses all test frames indiscriminately, anomalous frames will corrupt the memory, degrading performance. If it filters first, it needs a prior classifier — which is what the system is supposed to provide.

The separateness and compactness losses have competing objectives: separateness pushes memory items apart while compactness pulls features toward items. There is no evaluation of whether these losses actually produce better memory item diversity versus simply redistributing reconstruction error. The interaction between these two losses during training is not analyzed.

AUC on standard video anomaly detection benchmarks (UCSD Ped, CUHK Avenue) has known ceiling effects near 0.90+, making incremental improvements statistically uninformative. Temporal consistency of frame-level anomaly scores is not evaluated, which is the operationally relevant property for video surveillance applications.
all_issues_raised: The hybrid PSNR+L2 abnormality score has no stated weighting scheme, and performance sensitivity to the relative contribution of each component is not evaluated.
The test-time memory update strategy has a circular dependency: updating memory on normal test frames requires distinguishing normal from anomalous before the system has classified them.

The separateness and compactness losses have competing objectives with no analysis of their interaction or evidence that they produce better memory item diversity rather than simply redistributing reconstruction error.
AUC on standard video anomaly benchmarks has ceiling effects near 0.90+; temporal consistency of anomaly scores across frames is not evaluated despite being the operationally relevant metric.

The separateness and compactness losses have competing objectives with no analysis of their interaction or evidence that they produce better memory item diversity rather than simply redistributing reconstruction error.
